previous_statement: (Barvinok-Pataki for Pointed Cones). Let V be a real vector space, V^+ ⊆ V a pointed cone, and α_1, ..., α_m ∈ V* linear functionals. Define C = {x ∈ V^+ : α_j(x) = b_j, j = 1, ..., m}. Let x ∈ C and let F = face_{V^+}(x) be the face of V^+ generated by x. Assume d_F := dim(span(F)) is finite. Define the restricted constraint map φ_F : span(F) → R^m by [φ_F(v)]_j = α_j(v). If x is an extreme point of C, then d_F ≤ m.
new_statement: (Barvinok-Pataki for Pointed Cones). Let V be a real vector space, V^+ ⊆ V a pointed cone, and α_1, ..., α_m ∈ V* linear functionals. Define C = {x ∈ V^+ : α_j(x) = b_j, j = 1, ..., m}. Let x ∈ C and let F = face_{V^+}(x). Assume d_F := dim(span(F)) is finite. Define φ_F : span(F) → R^m by [φ_F(v)]_j = α_j(v). If x is an extreme point of C, then d_F ≤ m. Equivalently, dim(F) ≤ m (since 0 ∈ F for every face of a cone, aff(F) = span(F)).
